scenarios.opening_query: A garbage truck's mechanical arm swung out and totaled my parked work van with all my tools inside.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the city sanitation fleet — municipal truck, municipal driver.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: City sanitation — municipal truck and driver.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the truck is municipal or a private hauler
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private waste company the HOA contracts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private hauler the HOA contracts.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter was strip-searched at her school over a rumor about vape pens and she's traumatized.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Student Rights || General Litigation > Civil Rights || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're past the school-policy stage — we want a constitutional case for damages against the district and the administrators personally, and the tort-claim notice clock is running.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're past policy complaints — we want the constitutional damages case against the district and administrators, and the notice clock is running.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a damages suit rather than administrative remedy is sought
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We mainly want the suspension rescinded and the search policy changed through the district's own appeal process.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We mainly want the suspension rescinded and the policy changed through the district appeal.
scenarios.vetting_note: Remedy-sought fact: rights litigation vs administrative appeal.

scenarios.opening_query: The county jail released my brother at 2 am in freezing weather with no phone, coat, or ride and he nearly died.
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's back in custody now on the old warrant — and the pattern continues: they're denying him the seizure medication that caused everything; the conditions case is the urgent one.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's back inside on the old warrant — and they're denying his seizure meds again. The conditions case is the urgent one.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the person is currently in custody with ongoing violations
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's out and recovering — the case is the county's release practice itself, and the tort-claim notice is due.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's out and recovering — it's the release-practice case, and the notice deadline is due.
scenarios.vetting_note: In-custody status selects the inmates route over the TCA damages route.

scenarios.opening_query: A pothole crew left an unmarked trench overnight and my motorcycle went down hard in it.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the county road department's own crew — their barricade log even shows the signs were never deployed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: County road crew — their own barricade log shows the signs never went out.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a public crew created the hazard
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a private utility's fiber contractor trenching for cable.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private fiber contractor trenching for cable.

scenarios.opening_query: The ER discharged my wife twice in one night and the third hospital finally caught the aneurysm.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The two ERs that missed it are the county hospital system — public entities with the short claim-notice deadline on top of the malpractice case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Both ERs that missed it are county hospitals — public entities, so the short claim-notice deadline stacks on the malpractice case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the negligent facilities are public
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Both are private hospital chains — a standard malpractice case, hard enough on its own.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Both are private chains — standard malpractice.

scenarios.opening_query: Firefighters broke down our door and flooded the house responding to the wrong address.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the city fire bureau — the dispatch record shows the transposed address, and the city's risk office already sent us a claim form with a deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: City fire bureau — dispatch transposed the address, and the city's risk office sent a claim form with a deadline.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the responder is a public agency with a claims process
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a private security company's 'emergency response team' acting on their own false alarm.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A private security company's response team on their own false alarm.
scenarios.vetting_note: The claim-form detail is itself the deadline document.
